svg.text: How BacTraq traces and renames clusters based on the previous run
Each run clusters samples independently at every SNP threshold, forming a dot-notation tree. BacTraq then
matches this run's clusters back to the provided history and carries a cluster's name forward across merges,
splits, and singleton departures — instead of a fresh, unrelated number every run.
Small gray text under each current-run name is what a history-blind re-clustering pass would have called it — same members, no relationship to history.
Worked example from example/prev_clusters.csv → example/new_run_cluster.csv, matching example/new_run_cluster_bactraq.log.
